scope: Representor forwarding, bridge/FDB/VLAN/TC offload, and lower QueueLease derivation source map

authority_root.required: fresh RepresentorForwardCap plus fresh lower QueueLease epoch for software forwarding; QueueControl/OffloadCap plus lower QueueLease epoch for hardware switch/offload programming

source_anchors.limitation: representor metadata and queue state are Linux-mutable and do not prove lower QueueLease derivation or revoke.
generic transmit queues on skb->dev and cannot by itself preserve representor-to-lower authority provenance.
bridge and switchdev marks are forwarding policy/duplication controls, not lower QueueLease authority.
TC/switchdev programming is QueueControl/Offload authority and cannot be represented as plain per-packet RepresentorForward.

required_invariants: No lower queue submit through representor without frozen RepresentorForward-to-lower QueueLease carrier
No representor netdev reachability as lower QueueLease
No bridge FDB hit as lower QueueLease
No VLAN allow decision as lower QueueLease
No TC/switchdev hardware rule install without QueueControl/OffloadCap and lower QueueLease binding
No lower queue submit after LAG lower_dev change without metadata and lower QueueLease rebind
No representor forwarding or hardware rule forwarding after revoke
No representor queue stop as lower QueueLease revoke
forbidden_claims: representor netdev access authorizes lower queue submit
bridge FDB/VLAN success proves lower queue authority
TC redirect target dev proves lower queue authority
TC flower offload is only RepresentorForward and not QueueControl
LAG lower_dev rewrite preserves lower QueueLease without rebind
stopping representor queues revokes lower queue/DMA/IOMMU/IRQ authority
